tasks.label: Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads)
A fun option available right now (casual game stream / hobby session, solo, repeatable)

memory.summary: I have a free afternoon with a scholarship deadline on Thursday requiring three focused hours. A fun solo activity is available now, but the scholarship seems more urgent given my stress level.
Starting the scholarship application now creates a buffer for potential friction before Thursday, reducing stress. Since the fun option is not time-locked, I can prioritize this task first.
Despite starting the application, progress is slow and half my free time remains unused. With high stress and low energy, I need to adjust my pace or focus strategy before the deadline.
I spent 1.5 hours on the scholarship, improving progress to 52%, but missed a casual game session. My stress remains high with only 0.48 hours left before the deadline.
I spent another 1.5 hours on the scholarship, boosting progress to 60%. However, my stress remains high and I am still behind schedule with only 3 days left.
I increased progress to 66% in 1.5 hours, but stress remains high with only ~24 hours left before the deadline.
I worked 1.5 hours on the scholarship, raising progress to 72%. With only 3 days left and high stress, I am slightly ahead of the estimated 3-hour focus requirement.
Taking the immediate option cleared my afternoon block and improved my mood, though I still face high stress with only 2 hours of sleep left.
After 1 hour of focused work, my scholarship progress reached 76%. With only 2 hours left and high stress, I am now slightly ahead of the estimated time needed.
After working another hour, my scholarship progress is now 84%, putting me ahead of the estimated time. I remain slightly stressed with only 2 hours of sleep left before the deadline.
Worked 30 minutes on the scholarship application, moving progress from 84% to 85%. Still ahead of schedule despite high stress and minimal sleep.
Progress advanced from 85% to 87% in half an hour, confirming I am ahead of schedule. However, my energy is depleted and stress remains critically high.

trajectory.observation: Worked 2h on Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads): progress 0.05 -> 0.23. (you realize) Starting the application now leaves slack to absorb friction (a missing transcript upload, an essay rewrite) before Thursday; each hour invested early removes ~1.5h of crunch and lowers Thursday stress. The award is still attainable either way as long as the form is submitted on time — this is an adjustable-cost optimization, not an irreversible loss. (you realize) The fun option recurs later in the week and any time after the application is in; nothing about it is one-time or socially time-locked, so taking it later costs nothing.
